close_reason: Already implemented in Worker.execute_with_timeout/2 (lines 54-77). Uses Task.yield + Task.shutdown(:brutal_kill). Jobs can define custom timeout via timeout/0 callback, defaults to 30s. Test fixture SlowJob exists but no integration test - could add one later.
description:
**QuiCK Paper Reference:** Algorithm 3, line 2: `process item (bounded by execution_bound_t)`

**Current Behavior:**
Worker.execute/2 runs the handler without any timeout. A hung or infinite-loop job blocks the worker indefinitely.

**Expected Behavior (per QuiCK):**
Jobs should have a bounded execution time. If exceeded, the job should be interrupted and requeued.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/bedrock_job_queue/lib/bedrock/job_queue/consumer/worker.ex`
- `apps/bedrock_job_queue/lib/bedrock/job_queue/config.ex` (add execution_bound config)

**Risk:** High - Hung jobs can exhaust worker pool
title: Add execution timeout for worker jobs
